failure_surface: Formulas applied to target cells contain invalid syntax (extra & character before XLOOKUP function)
root_cause: Agent introduced an extraneous '&' operator in the formula string construction, creating '=IF(...,"",&XLOOKUP(...))' instead of '=IF(...,"",XLOOKUP(...))'. This invalid formula syntax would cause Excel to reject the cell values as errors.
minimal_fix_hypothesis: Before assigning any formula string to a cell, validate that the formula does not contain stray operators or characters between commas and function names. Specifically, ensure no '&' appears before built-in functions in formula strings unless explicitly required by Excel formula rules.
evidence: Excel formula syntax does not permit '&' prefix before function calls unless performing concatenation, which was not intended here
Evaluation result shows test_case_results: [0] indicating complete failure, consistent with malformed formulas that Excel cannot parse
Agent exploration phase correctly identified the data structure but introduced error during formula generation phase

memory_items.title: Validate Formula Syntax Before Assignment
memory_items.lesson: Always inspect constructed formula strings to ensure no extraneous operators appear where they do not belong, especially before function names.
memory_items.generalization: When programmatically generating Excel formulas in Python, review the final string pattern against standard formula syntax before assignment. Extra characters like '&', '+', or whitespace at critical positions can invalidate otherwise logical formulas.

memory_items.title: Confirm Function Availability Before Use
memory_items.lesson: Functions like XLOOKUP require specific Excel versions; use more compatible alternatives (VLOOKUP, INDEX/MATCH) when version support is uncertain.
memory_items.generalization: Before embedding modern Excel functions in generated formulas, verify that the target environment supports them. Consider fallback strategies or explicitly document version requirements.

memory_items.title: Test Formula Generation Independently
memory_items.lesson: Generate and display example formulas for human inspection before committing them to multiple cells.
memory_items.generalization: When looping to apply formulas to multiple cells, print at least one example formula string to the console to verify correctness before bulk assignment. This catches string formatting issues early.

edits.content:
- **Formula Syntax Validation**: After constructing a formula string, verify it matches standard Excel syntax before assigning to cells. Remove extraneous operators (like '&') that may be introduced during string interpolation. Print example formulas for verification when generating formulas programmatically.
- **Function Compatibility**: Prefer widely-supported functions (VLOOKUP, INDEX/MATCH) over newer functions (XLOOKUP, FILTER) unless version compatibility is confirmed, as these vary across Excel installations.
